channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Pokemon Champions Release Date Set for Early April
item.content:encoded:
The Pokemon Company has announced the Pokemon Champions release date, and it's coming to both of the Switch family consoles pretty soon, although mobile players are going to have to wait a little longer.

As revealed in a news post on the official Pokemon website, Pokemon Champions will be arriving on April 8th for both Switch 2 and Switch. As a reminder, it'll be a "free-to-start" game, which means you'll be able to get into it without paying, but some features are going to cost you.

Alongside the game, a Starter Pack is also launching, and it includes goodies like an additional piece of battle music, extra Pokemon storage, and more. If you're playing on Switch 2, you'll also be able to download a day one update to improve Pokemon Champions' visuals.

In Pokemon Champions, you'll be able to battle either with Pokemon included as part of the game or with your own team carried across from Pokemon Home. Naturally, not every Pokemon is in Champions, so only the ones that appear can be carried over.

Pokemon Champions' roster includes iconic critters like Dragonite, Scizor, and Altaria, and you can also battle with the Mega Evolved versions of Pokemon Legends: Z-A's starter Pokemon Feraligatr, Emboar, and Meganium.

Using Victory Points accumulated over the course of Pokemon Champions, you can train your Pokemon's specific stats, buy new outfits for your Trainer, and more.

You can check out a new Overview trailer for Pokemon Champions, which gives you a sense of what you can expect from the free-to-start battler, right here.

News of Pokemon Champions' release date comes shortly after Nintendo and The Pokemon Company revealed Pokemon Winds and Waves, the next mainline installments in the series, which are due to arrive on Switch 2 sometime next year.

While you wait for that, you can check out Pokemon Champions when it lands on Switch 2 and Switch on April 8th, and on mobile devices at some point in the future. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
